whenever.en: at any time that; no matter when

ex.en: Call me whenever you need help.

wherever.en: in or to any place that; no matter where

ex.en: You can sit wherever you like.

whether.cue: 두 가지 가능성 중 불확실함을 나타낼 때
whether.en: used to express a doubt or choice between two possibilities

ex.en: I don't know whether she will come.

whichever.en: any one that you choose, no matter which

ex.en: Take whichever seat you want.

whisper.en: speak very softly using your breath, without vibrating your vocal cords

ex.en: She whispered the secret in my ear.
ex.ko: 그녀는 내 귀에 대고 비밀을 속삭였다.

whisper.en: a soft, quiet way of speaking

ex.en: I heard a whisper from the next room.

whistle.en: make a clear musical sound by forcing breath through your lips

ex.en: He whistled a happy tune.

whistle.en: a small device you blow into to make a loud, sharp sound

ex.en: The referee blew his whistle.

whoever.en: any person that; no matter who

ex.en: Whoever finishes first wins a prize.
ex.ko: 누구든 먼저 끝내는 사람이 상을 받는다.

widespread.en: existing or happening in many places or among many people

ex.en: There is widespread support for the new law.
ex.ko: 그 새로운 법에 대한 지지가 널리 퍼져 있다.

wildlife.en: animals living in their natural environment, not kept by people

ex.en: The park protects local wildlife.

wildly.en: in an uncontrolled, excited, or violent manner

ex.en: The crowd cheered wildly when the team scored.
The movie was wildly popular.

willingness.en: the state of being ready or eager to do something

ex.en: She showed a willingness to help.

windscreen.en: the window at the front of a car (British English)

ex.en: A stone cracked the windscreen.

windsurfing.cue: 파도 위에서 돛단 보드를 타는 운동
windsurfing.en: a water sport in which you stand on a board with a sail and are moved by the wind

ex.en: They went windsurfing on the lake.

wing.en: one of the parts of a bird, insect, or plane that is used for flying

ex.en: The eagle spread its wings.

wing.en: a part of a building that sticks out from the main part

ex.en: The hospital built a new wing for children.
ex.ko: 병원은 어린이를 위한 새 별관을 지었다.

wing.en: a group within an organization that has particular views

ex.en: He belongs to the liberal wing of the party.
An electrician wired the new house.
ex.ko: 전기 기술자가 새 집에 배선을 했다.

wire.en: a thin piece of metal used to carry electricity or to fasten things

ex.en: The wire was hidden behind the wall.
ex.ko: 그 전선은 벽 뒤에 숨겨져 있었다.

wither.en: (of a plant) become dry and shrink, usually because it is dying

ex.en: The flowers withered in the heat.

witness.en: a person who sees something happen and can describe it

ex.en: Police are looking for witnesses to the accident.
ex.ko: 경찰이 그 사고의 목격자를 찾고 있다.

ex.en: She witnessed the whole event.

wizard.en: a person, especially in stories, who has magic powers

ex.en: The wizard cast a powerful spell.

wizard.en: someone who is extremely skilled at something

ex.en: She's a wizard with numbers.

working.en: having a job; actively doing paid work

ex.en: Most working adults commute every day.
We finally found a working printer.

working.cue: 실제로 쓸 수 있을 정도의(지식·관계 등)
working.en: good enough to be useful in a practical way

ex.en: I have a working knowledge of Spanish.
ex.ko: 나는 스페인어를 실무에 쓸 수 있는 정도로 안다.

workout.en: a period of physical exercise to keep fit

ex.en: I do a workout every morning.
Safety is important in every workplace.

workshop.en: a place where things are made or repaired, often by hand

ex.en: He fixes bikes in his workshop.

workshop.en: a meeting where people learn about or practice a subject together

ex.en: She attended a writing workshop last weekend.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지난 주말 글쓰기 워크숍에 참석했다.

worn.en: damaged or thin because of long use

ex.en: He wore a pair of worn jeans.

worn.en: looking tired because of hard work, worry, or illness

ex.en: Her face looked worn after the long journey.
ex.ko: 긴 여행 후 그녀의 얼굴은 초췌해 보였다.

worth.en: deserving your time, money, or effort

ex.en: This museum is definitely worth visiting.
ex.ko: 이 박물관은 분명 방문할 가치가 있다.

worth.en: having a particular value, especially in money

ex.en: This painting is worth a million dollars.
ex.ko: 이 그림은 백만 달러의 가치가 있다.

worth.en: the importance or value of something or someone

ex.en: She proved her worth to the team.

worthwhile.en: important, useful, or good enough to be worth the time or effort spent

ex.en: Learning a new language is worthwhile.
ex.ko: 새로운 언어를 배우는 것은 보람 있는 일이다.

worthy.en: good enough to deserve attention, respect, or a particular title

ex.en: It was a worthy effort even though they lost.

wound.en: injure someone's body, often by cutting or breaking the skin

ex.en: The soldier was wounded in the leg.

wound.en: an injury to the body, especially one with a cut or break in the skin

ex.en: The nurse cleaned the wound carefully.

wound.en: a painful emotional experience that has a lasting effect

ex.en: The insult left a deep wound on her pride.
ex.ko: 그 모욕은 그녀의 자존심에 깊은 상처를 남겼다.

wrap.en: cover something by folding paper, cloth, or other material around it

ex.en: She wrapped the gift in colorful paper.

wrap.en: a type of sandwich made by rolling food in a soft flatbread

ex.en: I had a chicken wrap for lunch.

wrap.en: a piece of clothing, such as a shawl, wrapped around the body

ex.en: She put a warm wrap over her shoulders.

wrapping.en: the paper or material used to cover and protect something

ex.en: He tore off the wrapping excitedly.

wrist.en: the joint connecting the hand to the arm

ex.en: She wore a bracelet on her wrist.

wristwatch.en: a watch worn on a strap around the wrist

ex.en: He checked the time on his wristwatch.

written.en: expressed in writing rather than spoken

ex.en: You must submit a written request.

yell.en: shout loudly, especially because you are angry, excited, or in pain

ex.en: He yelled at the driver to stop.
She let out a yell of excitement.

yoga.en: a system of exercises for the body and mind, often including breathing and meditation

ex.en: She practices yoga every morning.
